title: Korea Republic at the 2026 World Cup: roster, squad, players, group and schedule
original_text: Korea Republic head into their second 2026 FIFA World Cup group match against co-hosts Mexico under the guidance of manager Hong Myung-bo. Following a crucial opening victory over Czechia, the Taeguk Warriors boast a top-heavy squad anchored by world-class experience like Son Heung-min and Kim Min-jae, but they face a hostile atmosphere and a tactical litmus test in Guadalajara as they fight to secure an early ticket to the knockout rounds. World Cup 2026 calculator: simulate groups and matchups and generate your World Cup bracket prediction Drawn into a demanding Group A alongside co-hosts Mexico, Czechia, and South Africa, the Taegeuk Warriors possess a distinct “spear and shield” dynamic. Their defensive line is anchored by Bayern Munich’s elite center-back Kim Min-jae, while the creative spark and goal-scoring threat rests heavily upon the shoulders of Paris Saint-Germain’s Lee Kang-in and their iconic, talismanic captain Son Heung-min (now playing in MLS for LAFC). While Korea Republic have the elite individual quality to upset anyone on their day, their primary challenge lies in squad depth and tactical consistency; heavy pre-tournament friendly defeats have exposed defensive fragilities, meaning their chances of reaching the knockout rounds will rely entirely on their star trio firing in perfect unison. Thursday, June 11 Korea Republic 2-2 Czechia Thursday, June 18 Mexico 1-0 Korea Republic Wednesday, June 24 South Africa vs. Korea Republic Check out the World Cup rosters for all the other 47 teams at World Cup 2026 Get closer to the game!
